channel.title: Za Ndani
channel.description: Kenya and World News, Politics, Sports, and Entertainment

item.description: Willis Raburu is running for the Kisumu Senator seat to drive development in his hometown. The media personality shares his vision. Read the full story.
item.content:encoded: Willis Raburu is running for the Kisumu Senator seat to drive development in his hometown. The media personality shares his vision. Read the full story.

item.title: Meta reports revenue growth as daily active users decline
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Muthoni
item.description: African Tech Navigates Meta's Evolving Digital Landscape Meta's first quarter of 2026 presents a complex picture of robust revenue growth and strategic AI investment, tempered by declining user numbers and substantial losses in its Reality Labs division. These...

item.title: Umulkheir Harun addresses the escalating border conflict and banditry in Kitui
item.dc:creator: Zed Mogaka
item.description: Umulkheir Harun addresses the surge in banditry and fatalities in Kitui, clarifying tensions between Kamba farmers and Somali camel herders in Nguni Ward.

item.title: How Nairobi Floods Paralysed Mombasa Road and JKIA

item.title: Inside NTSA Crackdown on Drug Use in School Vehicles

item.title: Inside Court Ruling on Kenya-US Health Data Agreement

item.title: Kenya to Extend Mandatory Alcohol Tracking to Curb KSh 120 Billion Tax Loss
item.dc:creator: Grace Mkamburi
item.description: Curbing Illicit Alcohol to Recover KSh 120 Billion in Tax Revenue Kenya plans to extend mandatory excise stamps to all alcoholic beverages, including second-generation brews, to curb an estimated KSh 120 billion annual tax loss from illicit trade. This...
item.content:encoded: Curbing Illicit Alcohol to Recover KSh 120 Billion in Tax Revenue Kenya plans to extend mandatory excise stamps to all alcoholic beverages, including second-generation brews, to curb an estimated KSh 120 billion annual tax loss from illicit trade. This...

item.title: Confirmed: Mike Sonko Acquitted in Money Laundering Case
item.dc:creator: Celestine Nzioka
item.description: Nairobi court acquits Mike Sonko of money laundering charges due to lack of evidence but orders him to defend irregular tenders. Read the full story.
item.content:encoded: Nairobi court acquits Mike Sonko of money laundering charges due to lack of evidence but orders him to defend irregular tenders. Read the full story.

item.title: Confirmed: IG Kanja Secures Nairobi-Garissa Highway

item.content:encoded: Inspector General Douglas Kanja has deployed GSU and RDU units to the Nairobi-Garissa Highway to stop criminal attacks. Read the full story.

item.title: Confirmed: Kenya electricity prices to drop as hydro dams fill
item.dc:creator: Celestine Nzioka
item.description: KenGen signals a drop in Kenya electricity prices for homes and firms as water levels rise in major hydro dams nationwide. Get the full details now.
item.content:encoded: KenGen signals a drop in Kenya electricity prices for homes and firms as water levels rise in major hydro dams nationwide. Get the full details now.

item.title: Heavy rains submerge Nairobi estates and render major roads impassable
item.dc:creator: Za Ndani

item.title: Somali businesses looted in Mwingi town as retaliatory violence escalates in Kitui County

item.content:encoded: Mwingi town residents looted Somali businesses on April 29, 2026, as retaliatory violence between the Kamba and Somali communities intensifies in Kitui.

item.title: Liz Jackson highlights harassment of women in Nairobi Central Business District

item.content:encoded: Liz Jackson highlights the daily physical harassment of women in Nairobi’s CBD, sharing her personal experiences and calling the city center ungovernable.

item.title: Senator Allan Chesang advocates for fair revenue sharing for Kenyan content creators in the Senate
